items.title: XRP Ledger’s new upgrade is here. But not everyone’s on board yet
items.description: The new software leads among the network's validators, yet by node count it sits behind the older v3.1.3, and the security amendment bundled with it remains a separate, slower vote. The upgrade needs 80% of the trusted validator list to activate.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin drops as yen, Iran ceasefire collapse
items.description: Hedge funds have turned the most bearish on the yen since 2007, boosting bets on further losses to nearly 138,000 contracts as of June 30.

items.title: Bitcoin under pressure as Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
items.description: Major cryptocurrencies are trading in the red as renewed U.S.-Iran airstrikes push oil higher.

items.title: BlackRock-backed Securitize slides 40% after SPAC debut despite tokenization boom
items.description: The decline fits into a pattern of recently-public digital asset companies sliding after debut, Arca's Jeff Dorman said.

items.title: Crypto exchange Kraken is trying to become a bank in Europe
items.description: Kraken's focus is on Lithuania as the jurisdiction to secure the license, according to a person familiar with the plans.

items.title: SpaceX IPO powers record $3.86 billion in tokenized equities trading in June
items.description: SpaceX tokens captured $1.19 billion, or 31% of total monthly volume, with Backpack’s SPCX leading trading at $1.08 billion.

items.title: U.S. SEC to propose crypto rule as soon as this month to ease startups, fundraising
items.description: A newly updated agenda from the securities regulator shows so-called Reg Crypto high on its near-term list.

items.title: Analysts see more upside for SpaceX as post-IPO research begins
items.description: Major brokerages initiated coverage on Tuesday with mostly buy ratings following the company's $75 billion June IPO.

items.title: Base to activate B20 standard for stablecoins, RWAs and other tokens
items.description: Base is scheduled to activate its B20 token standard on Wednesday at 6 pm UTC, allowing developers to begin creating native tokens on the network.

items.title: EU to again vote to extend ‘chat control’ rules
items.description: So-called “chat control” rules could be revived in the EU, with lawmakers again set to vote on the legislation Thursday.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: StarkWare CEO suggests 4% annual Bitcoin inflation to replace 21M cap
items.description: StarkWare CEO Eli Ben-Sasson argued that Bitcoin private keys get lost over time, meaning the amount of usable Bitcoin will diminish. Many disagree.

items.title: Swyftx eyes crypto payments after securing Australian license
items.description: Swyftx interim co-CEO Andrea Yuen says the company "won’t be a pure crypto spot exchange in future,” after it secured a license allowing it to offer payment services.

items.title: Secret Network cites AI exploit risks in proposed Arbitrum move
items.description: “The security risk is the part we take most seriously,” the team said, citing old code and AI exploit risks.

items.title: CFTC charges commodity, crypto pool operator with $14M fraud
items.description: The CFTC has launched a rare crypto-related enforcement action against a commodity pool operator that allegedly defrauded investors of more than $14 million.

items.title: Strike launches ‘volatility-proof’ Bitcoin loans amid bear market, but at a cost
items.description: The cost of eliminating margin calls and forced liquidations is an interest rate as high as 14.2% and an obligation to pay on time, Strike CEO Jack Mallers said.

items.title: Oil surges 6% after Trump says ceasefire with Iran is over
items.description: "To me, I think it's over," Trump said of the ceasefire with Iran. "I don't want to deal with them anymore."

items.title: Trump says he doesn't want anything to do with Spain: 'Cut off all trade'
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump slammed Spain for not contributing enough to NATO, as he attended the defense alliance summit in Turkey.

items.title: NATO allies put on a united front as they meet Trump
items.description: NATO chief Mark Rutte said Wednesday that the alliance was entering its "NATO 3.0" era.

items.title: Trump doubles down on push for control over Greenland as Denmark vows to defend it
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the U.S. needs to control Greenland “for the protection of the world."

items.title: China warns about AI risks with Anthropic's Claude Code
items.description: China said specific versions of Claude Code posed back-door vulnerabilities that could send sensitive information to a remote server.

items.title: SambaNova hits $11 billion valuation as investors back Nvidia chip challengers
items.description: SambaNova is valued at $11 billion after fresh financing led by General Atlantic, as AI chip startups look to challenge Nvidia.

items.title: Lawmakers probe growing use of Chinese AI models in U.S. companies
items.description: An ongoing House Committee investigation is probing the risks involved in the rise of AI built in China.

items.title: Samsung-backed AI chip firm Rebellions targets IPO in South Korea next year, CEO tells CNBC
items.description: Sunghyun Park, CEO of Rebellions, told CNBC that the company is leaning toward a listing on the KOSPI over the KOSDAQ.

Bitcoin’s ongoing bear market has been ’rather comforting,’ analyst says

Bitcoin trades above $62K as U.S. crypto bill gains fresh traction

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=62e69Bz7-ww! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Dakota Johnson quickly sells $6 million midcentury modern home in L.A.
items.description: “Fifty Shades” star Dakota Johnson has officially sold her stunning Los Angeles home for just a few dollars under her $6 million asking price—just days after putting it on the market.

items.title: After selling her $5 million Westchester home, Drew Barrymore shows off her RV
items.description: Drew Barrymore is going back to basics for her summer getaway this year—revealing that she is ready to take an adventure in an RV.

items.title: Alibaba just had its best day in 10 months. Is it time for China techs to catch up?
items.description: Alibaba on Wednesday saw its best single share-price gain in 10 months, as the broader Chinese tech sector revved higher after missing the rally propelling U.S., Korean and Taiwanese companies.

items.title: It was the world’s hottest stock market. Now South Korea’s stock market has entered bear-market territory
items.description: The cumulative impact of several different narratives – competition, sustainability, excessive leverage, international outflows- has sapped the Korean market of its upward mobility since clocking up a record high in June.

items.title: Plot twist: Gen Z is buying houses after all. Here’s how they’re doing it.
items.description: Despite soaring housing costs, younger buyers are finding a way in.

items.title: Rivian’s stock sale triggers worst rout for the shares in nearly two years
items.description: Rivian’s stock falls 18% as Wall Street frets about the EV maker’s cash troubles.

items.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
items.description: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother.”

items.title: Micron’s stock falls as investors wonder if the memory market is near the top
items.description: “Most investor feedback continues to point to a skittish AI tape,” one analyst said.

Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cVB5Uy_OoZI! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: UniCredit secures 48% stake in Commerzbank
items.description: Italian bank closing in on majority control of German lender after end of tender period

items.title: Re-election would not put Farage in the clear
items.description: Opposition parties refuse to stand against scandal-hit politician in Reform’s safest seat of Clacton

items.title: US launches wave of Iran strikes after attacks on tankers
items.description: Donald Trump says he thinks ceasefire deal with Tehran is ‘over’ as Revolutionary Guards retaliate

items.title: Farage by-election gambit undercut by rivals’ refusal to participate
items.description: Conservatives, Labour and Lib Dems will not contest vote as Reform UK leader denies wrongdoing over gifts

items.title: Virgin Media fined £28mn for preventing customers from cancelling contracts
items.description: Ofcom uncovers delay tactics that meant millions of calls were likely to have been mishandled

items.title: South Korea falls into bear market as traders fret over AI chipmakers’ prospects
items.description: Kospi retreats more than 20% from June peak as sentiment starts to turn on Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix

items.title: Singapore’s Temasek doubles down on AI and private credit
items.description: State investor focuses on accelerating returns after several lacklustre years

items.title: New Zealand central bank chief hails growth ‘rebound’
items.description: RBNZ raises interest rates for first time in 3 years as inflation persists

items.title: A New ‘Little House’ Expands and Updates the Prairie
items.description: A Netflix adaptation of the beloved tale empowers Ma and adds new Black and Native characters to challenge prejudices.The creators know criticism is coming.

items.title: EU Rejects Plea to Pause Troubled EES Biometrics System at Airports Ahead of Summer Rush
items.description: E.U. leaders rebuffed a plea from the aviation industry to pause the biometric checkpoints, which have caused long lines and missed flights. Summer travelers beware.

items.title: Oil Prices Jump After U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes in Persian Gulf
items.description: Attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz sparked a new cycle of retaliation that could throttle the flow of energy from the region.

items.title: Germans Don’t Love Budweiser. It Won’t Take No for an Answer.
items.description: Not for the first time, Budweiser is trying to make it in Germany. But this is a tough time to sell German drinkers on the image and taste of American beer.

items.title: Is ‘The View’ a News Show? ABC Says That’s Already Settled.
items.description: In a new filing, ABC said that the F.C.C. had ruled in 2002 that “The View” was a “bona fide news program.”

items.title: Mariska Hargitay to Host the Emmys
items.description: The “Law & Order: SVU” star is a departure from the comedians and sitcom actors who have typically hosted the annual awards show.

items.title: Trump Promised a Foreign Investment Boom. It’s Getting Harder to Deliver.
items.description: The threat of tariffs may have helped push more capital into the United States, but other factors are pushing it away.

items.title: U.S. Trade Deficit Widens in May on Record Goods Imports
items.description: Imports of foreign goods, including pharmaceuticals and equipment for data centers, hit a record high, pushing the monthly trade deficit to its highest level in over a year.
